other_observers: Nathan Pieplow, Andrew Spencer, Nick Komar, Eric Zorawowicz, Bill Prather, Inez Prather; I am not the original finder of the bird. The original finder is Mark Miller.
specific_location: Where Left Hand Creek crosses Pike Rd. in Longmont 40.137816N 105.117477W
description: Overall, the bird had a dark back and wings with white undersides showing dark longitudinal streaking. The bird had a broad white supercilium which did not taper posterior to the birds eye. It's throat was white and unmarked and bordered by dark malar stripes. The flanks showed a faint salmon colored tinge which extended posteriorly towards the bird's vent. The bird also had bright pink legs.Behaviors: The bird was observed voraciously eating small invertebrates amongst the rocks in the stream. The bird constantly bobbed its tail in an exaggerated U shape with the bobbing motion involving nearly the birds entire body.Call: NonePlumage: Spring Adult
